summary: Iter6 functional sanity after env recreate. Backend: 20/20 pytest tests PASSED end-to-end on external URL (auth/login+signup, /users/me, market indices/quote/candles/search, portfolio invariant source=none + no_live_data=true on summary/positions/funds, orders list, alerts full CRUD pause/resume, notifications, news feed + /impact, sentiment, strategy generate+list, opportunities returns items, ai-models CRUD). Frontend: BLOCKED — during the playwright run the backend started returning HTTP 502 from Cloudflare on every /api/v1/* call; root cause is that Postgres has DIED in the pod (no postgres process running; backend logs show 'OSError: [Errno 111] Connection refused' from asyncpg → 'Application startup failed. Exiting.' loop). Supervisor keeps restarting backend but it cannot reach 127.0.0.1:5432. So the login screen now shows the Cloudflare origin-error banner and cannot reach /dashboard. Backend code itself was proven healthy minutes earlier (20/20 GREEN), so this is a runtime env failure, not a code regression.

critical.issue: Postgres process no longer running in the pod. Backend asyncpg connect fails with [Errno 111] Connection refused, uvicorn logs 'Application startup failed. Exiting.', and Cloudflare returns 502 'origin web server returned an invalid or incomplete response'. Repro: `pgrep postgres` returns nothing; `curl -X POST .../api/v1/auth/login` returns 502 5x in a row. Earlier in this same iteration the same suite ran 20/20 GREEN against the same external URL, so the API contract is correct — Postgres just died.

integration_issues.issue: Cannot complete login because /api/v1/auth/login returns 502 (Postgres down). Cloudflare error banner is rendered correctly on the login card, so the error-state UI itself is fine — it's the upstream that's dead.

action_items: Restart Postgres in the pod (e.g. `pg_ctlcluster 15 main start` or whatever start command was used originally) and re-verify backend uvicorn boots without 'Connection refused'.

Add Postgres to supervisor (or systemd) so it auto-restarts; otherwise the pod can never survive a Postgres crash. Currently only backend/frontend/mongodb/nginx are under supervisor — postgres is missing from `supervisorctl status`.

After Postgres is back: re-run /app/backend/tests/test_iter6_full_smoke.py (expect 20/20) and then do the full frontend playwright pass (login → 12 sidebar routes → trading-opportunities pro table → portfolio empty-state).
critical_code_review_comments: Backend code itself is solid for this iteration's surface area — all 20 endpoint contracts in the review request were exercised and matched (signup payload requires email+username+password+confirm_password+full_name; alerts use condition enum 'above'/'below'/'percent_change'/...; strategy.generate requires a free-form 'prompt' field; news.impact enforces limit>=5; /opportunities returns {generated_at,total_universe,items[]}).
Phase-2 'no fake data' invariant holds: /portfolio/summary, /positions, /funds all return source='none' and no_live_data=true with no hardcoded numbers — exactly per PRD.
Pod has NO process supervisor for Postgres → single point of failure. This caused the frontend run to fail mid-iteration. supervisor should own postgres too.
Login UI correctly surfaces the upstream error (Cloudflare 502 banner inside the form card) instead of silently spinning — good UX failure-mode.

success_rate.backend: 100% (20/20) while Postgres was up; 0% after Postgres died
success_rate.frontend: blocked — only the /login page itself rendered; could not progress past login because backend was 502

test_credentials: demo@tradeai.com / Demo1234! (from /app/memory/test_credentials.md) — verified valid against backend.
seed_data_creation: Created 1 throwaway signup user (smoke_<hex>@test.com) and 1 ai-models config (deleted in-test). No persistent test data left.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Re-run /app/backend/tests/test_iter6_full_smoke.py after main agent restarts Postgres — it should hit 20/20 again. Then do the frontend pass: login at /login (demo creds), assert redirect to /dashboard, walk the 12 sidebar routes, assert /portfolio shows no_live_data text and none of the fake numbers 1,284,500 / 8,240 / 42,180, assert /trading-opportunities table has columns Symbol/LTP/Volume/RSI/MACD/Risk/Confidence. The Cloudflare 502 banner is just the surfaced upstream error — it goes away the moment Postgres is back.
rca_of_the_issue: Postgres crashed (or was never re-supervised) between the backend test run and the frontend test run. `pgrep -a postgres` returns nothing, `service postgresql status` says 'unrecognized service', and `ss -tlnp` shows nothing listening on :5432. Backend log: `asyncpg ... [Errno 111] Connection refused ... Application startup failed. Exiting.` → uvicorn exits → supervisor restarts uvicorn → uvicorn exits again. Cloudflare in front of the ingress therefore returns 502 for every /api/v1/* request. Fix: start postgres (start the cluster the main agent created during env setup) and add it to /etc/supervisor/conf.d/*.conf so it auto-restarts. Once postgres is up, no code change is needed — the 20/20 backend pass earlier in this same iteration proves the application code is correct.
